(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary: Woke up early on this gorgeous sunny Monday feeling energized. The bike ride to Capitol Hill was perfect—wind in my face, coffee waiting at Espresso Vivace. Ended up catching the USA vs Wales match on their TV while sketching, which was a nice distraction. The energy at Pike Place was chaotic in the best way; grabbed lunch at Matt's and lost an hour digging through vinyl at Sonic Boom. Skateboarded down to Pioneer Square on a whim and got completely absorbed in the galleries—there's this one artist doing these incredible mixed-media pieces that really spoke to me. Made it back to Cap Hill for dinner at Stateside, then headed to Q Nightclub for the live show. The music was exactly what I needed. Felt like the whole day just flowed, you know? Like all the pieces clicked into place.
thought_process: I think the sunshine really set the tone—I woke up wanting to move, to explore, and the light rail closure actually worked in my favor since I could skateboard instead. Catching the World Cup match felt like a nice anchor point, something social happening in real time. After that, I just followed my curiosity: vinyl hunting, art galleries, live music. I realized partway through the day that I was chasing inspiration more than sticking to routine, and I leaned into it. The Pioneer Square galleries especially reminded me why I became an artist in the first place. By evening I was buzzing with ideas, so the live show felt like the perfect way to channel that energy.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
